Galactic ID — Physical Wiring Routing Map

Supplement to the Complete Electrical System Spec • Where each new wire physically runs inside the Card Creator booth

1. Annotated Interior — Zone Map

The Card Creator booth interior is organized into distinct zones. This section maps each new Galactic ID wire run to its physical location using the booth photos as reference.

Full interior — rear door open. Zone A (top): speaker enclosures, Teltonika modem, camera power adapter, "Advertising lamp" wire. Zone B (center): 19" monitor rear, Big Ring mounting ring. Zone C (mid-lower): Lepy amplifier, LED Controller, Pulse Interface, mini PC. Zone D (bottom): PSU bay, ePort, distribution/terminal block, power entry. QR code sticker on right door = "See Our Photoma Mini Manual".
Zone A — Speaker & Modem Area. L/R speaker enclosures (black boxes with sound ports). Teltonika modem (right, blue/yellow label). Speaker cable with blue Fakra connectors (center). "Advertising lamp" wire (far left, yellow/black). Galactic ID: Holo fan AC cord and antenna wires route up through ceiling into this zone.
Ceiling Panel (looking up from inside). Fan vent (red, center), reflective tape strips (top), existing wiring pass-throughs. Remove 8 screws to access for routing holo fan power cord and 4 antenna wires down into Zone A.
Zone C–D — PC, ePort, Terminal Block. Mini PC (center, velcro-strapped), ePort (bottom-left, blue LED), LED Controller (upper-right, green PCB), Pulse Interface (upper-right, red PCB). Terminal block visible at bottom-right with labeled wires. AC power switch (red, far left). Galactic ID: PSU 2 mounts in this zone. New 12V leads connect at terminal block.
PSU Bay (bottom-left of Zone D). Mean Well RSP-500 series 12V supply. Wire colors: yellow (12V), green/yellow (earth), red (5V bus), black (GND). This is PSU 1. PSU 2 will mount adjacent or below, with AC feed from the same power entry module.

2. Wire Routing — New Component by Component

1
Pill Panel Wiring Routing

Pill Panel R (Right Door) — from PSU 1

LED strips on wood backing (Apr 3 TSL). These arrive pre-wired from TSL with a single 12V lead pair exiting the panel. During installation, this lead routes from the panel through the door hinge area into the main electronics cabinet.
Terminal/distribution block at bottom of Zone D. Pill Panel R's 12V lead connects here — at the existing distribution block where PSU 1's 12V bus is available. Wire labels visible: GND, 12V, ALT. Use existing labeled terminals.
Physical routing: pill panel R

12V lead exits pill panel R (right door) → through door hinge wire channel → runs down right side of cabinet interior → terminates at distribution block (Zone D, bottom-right). Connects to PSU 1's 12V bus at the labeled "12V" terminal.

Pill Panel L (Left Door) — from PSU 2 (NEW)

Both pill panels lit on booth (Mar 31 TSL). The left door panel has the same single 12V lead pair. This lead routes through the left door hinge area into the cabinet — but connects to the new PSU 2 rather than the existing distribution block.
Left side of cabinet — AC power strip with labeled IEC plugs. PSU 2 mounts on the left wall or floor of the cabinet near the existing PSU 1 bay, with its AC input fed from the power strip. Pill Panel L's 12V lead connects directly to PSU 2's output terminals.
Physical routing: pill panel L

12V lead exits pill panel L (left door) → through left door hinge wire channel → runs down left side of cabinet interior → terminates at PSU 2 output terminals (new, mounted in Zone D left wall). PSU 2's AC input plugs into the AC power strip.

Both doors must be skinned with white vinyl on the interior surface behind the pill panels. This provides the reflective backing for even light diffusion through the milk acrylic lenses.
Panel cutout reference (Mar 10 TSL). Pill panel doors don't require body cutouts — they mount on the inner door surface. But the side panel and instrument panel do require cutouts into the booth body (shown in blue).
2
Side Panel Wiring Routing
Apr 3 TSL — Rear of side panel showing both LED tails. Tail 1 (12V white COB): connects to distribution block at Zone D. Tail 2 (WS2811 RGB + data): connects to new LED controller.
Existing LED Controller (green PCB) in Zone C. Has screw terminals for: Camera Bulb DIM, Exit Photo Lamp, Big Ring Color, Bottom Ring Color, etc. The new Galactic ID RGB controller will be mounted adjacent to this board, receiving Micro USB data from the PC and 12V/5V from the PSU bus.
Physical routing: side panel

Tail 1 (12V COB, red pill backlight): Exits side panel rear → routes through body via side panel cutout → runs along right wall of cabinet → terminates at distribution block (Zone D) on PSU 1's 12V bus.

Tail 2 (WS2811 RGB, ID backlight): Exits side panel rear → routes through body → runs to new LED controller (Zone C, mounted near existing controller). Data signal + power both connect at the controller.

3
Instrument Panel Wiring Routing
Apr 3 TSL — Instrument panel rear with Wago connector hub. All LEDs (COB strip + 8 indicator bulbs) join to a single 12V lead pair. This lead exits the rear of the panel through the 3 rectangular cutouts in the front fascia.
Zone C — Pulse Interface and LED Controller area. The instrument panel's single 12V lead routes behind the monitor ring frame and down to the distribution block in Zone D. Wire labels and zip ties follow existing cable management patterns.
Physical routing: instrument panel

Single 12V lead exits instrument panel Wago hub → routes through 3 rectangular cutouts in front fascia → runs behind monitor ring frame → down through Zone C → terminates at distribution block (Zone D) on PSU 1's 12V bus. Short, direct path.

4
Chute Border Wiring Routing
Existing print chute LED strip — already wired with labeled "Exit photo lamp" wire (yellow/black) running to the LED controller. Spliced with red wire nuts. This shows the existing wiring pattern the chute border's 9 new indicator bulbs will follow.
Existing "PRINTS HERE" backlit marquee on lower door. The new Galactic ID chute border mounts around this area — the vacuum-formed silver-gray covering with 9 indicator bulbs sits over/around the existing print output slot.
Physical routing: chute border

Single 12V lead from 9 indicator bulbs → follows the same routing as the existing "Exit photo lamp" / "Inside Print Chute" wires → runs along the lower door interior → through the hinge area → down to distribution block (Zone D). Very low power (0.18W), thin gauge wire sufficient.

5
Holo Fan Wiring Routing
Mar 17 TSL — Holo tower mounted on booth. The 65cm holographic fan sits inside the 3D-printed PLA tower housing. Its AC power cord exits the bottom of the tower assembly.
STEP file top view — cable routing paths. Holo fan AC extension cord follows the same path as the antenna wires (blue lines). Routes from the tower through one of the ceiling panel openings.
Ceiling panel interior — the fan power cord enters through one of the existing pass-through points (or a new hole near the fan vent center). Drops into the space above Zone A (speakers/modem area).
AC Power Strip (left side of cabinet). The 10-foot 90° extension cord runs from the ceiling down through Zone A, behind the speaker enclosures, and plugs into an available outlet on this strip. Fan's own PSU mounts anywhere in the electronics panel.
Physical routing: holo fan AC cord

Fan power cord exits holo tower base → through ceiling panel opening (requires removing 8 screws) → drops into space above Zone A → routes behind speaker enclosures → runs down left wall alongside existing AC power cables → plugs into AC power strip (left side of Zone D). Total run: ~8 feet of the 10-foot extension cord. Plenty of slack.

3. PSU 2 — Installation Details

Existing PSU 1 — Mean Well RSP-500 in lower-left bay. Note the mounting bracket and wire routing. PSU 2 should mount on the opposite wall or below PSU 1, using similar bracket hardware.
Zone C–D overview. Available mounting space for PSU 2: on the left wall below the AC power strip, or on the back wall below the mini PC (if the PC is shifted). A Mean Well LRS-150-12 is only 159×97×30mm — compact enough to mount almost anywhere.
PSU 2 Installation Checklist

1. Mount Mean Well LRS-150-12 (or LRS-200-12) in Zone D — left wall or below PSU 1 bracket.
2. Connect AC input to power strip (or wire to terminal block on power entry module).
3. Connect 12V output directly to Pill Panel L's lead pair (single connection point).
4. Add 5A slow-blow fuse on the 12V output per existing Card Creator wiring standard ("All secondary voltage lines have 5A slow-blow fuses").
5. Label: "PSU 2 — PILL PANEL L — GALACTIC ID".

4. Installation Wiring Checklist

#TaskFromToWire Type
1Mount PSU 2AC power stripZone D left wallIEC AC cord
2Pill Panel R → Distro BlockR door hinge channelDistribution block "12V" terminal12V DC, 18 AWG min
3Pill Panel L → PSU 2L door hinge channelPSU 2 output terminals12V DC, 18 AWG min
4Side Panel Tail 1 → Distro BlockSide panel cutoutDistribution block "12V" terminal12V DC, 22 AWG ok
5Side Panel Tail 2 → LED ControllerSide panel cutoutNew RGB controller (Zone C)Data + 12V, 3-wire
6Instrument Panel → Distro BlockFront fascia cutoutsDistribution block "12V" terminal12V DC, 22 AWG ok
7Chute Border → Distro BlockLower door hingeDistribution block "12V" terminal12V DC, 24 AWG ok
8Holo Fan Ext Cord → AC StripCeiling panel openingAC power strip outlet120V AC, 10ft ext cord
94× Antenna Wires → ModemCeiling panel (4 holes, 14mm)Teltonika modem (Zone A)Antenna coax
10White Vinyl Backer—Inner surface of both doors + booth side behind side panelWhite vinyl sheet
Wire Gauge Notes

Pill panels draw the most current (~7–11A each at 12V). Use 18 AWG minimum for these runs. Side panel, instrument panel, and chute border are low-current (under 2A) — 22–24 AWG is fine. All secondary DC lines should have 5A slow-blow fuses per existing Card Creator standard.
